description: Lecture on the concept of eventual liveness in blockchains, discussing its definition and implications for state machine replication. The speaker explains how liveness is affected by honest and Byzantine nodes, and the conditions under which transactions are expected to be added to local histories.
topic_summary: Exploration of eventual liveness in blockchain protocols, focusing on the conditions necessary for transactions to be included in local histories after global stabilization time [t=12:00]. The lecture addresses the impact of Byzantine nodes and the importance of having honest leaders in consecutive rounds [t=243:47].
